skill.name: nano-banana-edit
skill.description: Provides guidance for editing images with Google Nano Banana 2 through the RunComfy image-to-image edit endpoint. It covers model selection, input schema, prompt patterns, batch edits, output handling, and security notes for using the RunComfy CLI.
skill.summary: Edit images with Nano Banana 2 on RunComfy using structured prompts, batch inputs, and CLI-based output downloads.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis heavily over-counted Markdown backticks and examples as executable Ruby shell syntax, so most command findings are false positives. The skill still intentionally uses the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and image URLs to RunComfy over HTTPS, and writes outputs or tokens locally, so publication should include a medium-risk warning. No evidence of malware, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden execution was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Processing Through RunComfy
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to send prompts and image URLs to the RunComfy Model API through the local RunComfy CLI. This is expected behavior for the skill, but user images, prompts, and generated outputs leave the local environment and should be treated as third-party processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicitly documented, including the API endpoint and output download domains. It appears intentional rather than malicious, but it materially affects user privacy.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Required
medium_findings.description: The skill requires installing and running the RunComfy CLI, including commands for login and model execution. This is core functionality, but external command execution increases supply-chain and local execution risk compared with a pure documentation skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands are clear and legitimate for RunComfy usage. Risk remains because the workflow depends on installing and executing third-party CLI software.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage Documented
low_findings.description: The skill explains that RunComfy login stores an API token under the user configuration directory with owner-only permissions. This is not hidden malicious access, but users should understand where credentials are stored and may prefer RUNCOMFY_TOKEN in isolated environments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and permission mode are documented directly in the security section. The text describes normal CLI credential storage rather than unauthorized access.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal and Weak Crypto Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings are caused by placeholder HTTPS image URLs containing ellipses, and the weak cryptography findings appear to match model naming or headings rather than cryptographic code. No executable path traversal, cryptographic implementation, or system reconnaissance logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation, placeholders, and exit-code tables at the flagged locations. There is no code that traverses local paths or implements weak cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked External Command Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow combines local CLI execution with remote model API calls and output downloads. This is necessary for the skill, but users should only run it with trusted image URLs, expected output directories, and an authenticated RunComfy account.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and security notes clearly describe the external command and network boundaries. The pattern is operationally sensitive but not evidence of malicious intent.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Nano Banana on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Image editing workflows often lose identity, framing, or batch consistency. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Nano Banana Edit through RunComfy with structured prompts and clear routing rules.
actual_capabilities: Routes explicit Nano Banana Edit requests to the RunComfy image-to-image edit endpoint.
Documents the required input fields for prompts, image URLs, output count, seed, aspect ratio, resolution, and format.
Provides CLI invocation examples for single-image edits, batch edits, and targeted spatial edits.
Explains prompt patterns for preserving identity, localizing changes, and improving batch consistency.
Compares Nano Banana Edit with GPT Image 2 edit, Flux Kontext, and Nano Banana text-to-image use cases.
Documents RunComfy exit codes, token storage, network endpoints, and output download behavior.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and authenticated access before model calls can run.
Sends prompts and image URLs to RunComfy for remote processing.
Supports one to twenty input images and one to four outputs per call.
Long compound edit prompts can drift and should be split into smaller passes.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent Product Backgrounds
use_cases.description: Use batch image edits to place related product photos on matching backgrounds while keeping product identity and framing stable.

use_cases.title: Prepare Campaign Image Variants
use_cases.description: Generate several controlled image variations for ads, social posts, or landing pages with fixed resolution and repeatable prompt structure.

use_cases.title: Guide AI Agents Through RunComfy Edits
use_cases.description: Give Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a clear workflow for choosing the endpoint, building inputs, and handling RunComfy outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Background Swap
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single image edit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana Edit to keep the subject identity, pose, and clothing unchanged. Replace only the background with a clean studio setting.

prompt_templates.title: Localized Object Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that needs spatial control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the input image with Nano Banana Edit. Change only the object in the upper-right area, and keep every other object, shadow, and camera angle unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Product Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple related images.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Nano Banana Edit for this product batch. Keep each product shape, label, and perspective unchanged, then apply the same neutral background and locked square framing.

prompt_templates.title: Model Routing Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before choosing an image model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this edit request and decide whether Nano Banana Edit is the right RunComfy endpoint. Explain any reason to use GPT Image 2 edit, Flux Kontext, or Nano Banana text-to-image instead.

output_examples.input: A user asks to change a portrait background while keeping the person unchanged.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Nano Banana Edit, writes a preservation-first prompt, uses one image URL, and saves the generated result to the chosen output directory.

output_examples.input: A designer provides several product images that need the same studio background.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a batch edit, locks aspect ratio and resolution, and uses the same prompt grammar across every input image.

output_examples.input: A user wants text replacement on a multilingual sign inside an image.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that GPT Image 2 edit may be a better match because the skill notes stronger multilingual in-image text handling there.

best_practices: Start prompts with what must stay unchanged, then state the edit in one direct sentence.
Lock aspect ratio and resolution when editing a batch of related images.
Use trusted image URLs and review privacy needs before sending assets to RunComfy.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many unrelated edits into one long prompt.
Do not use vague instructions such as making the image better or more premium.
Do not enable web search unless the edit specifically needs grounding and the extra latency is acceptable.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to edit images with Google Nano Banana 2 through the RunComfy CLI and image edit endpoint.

faq.question: Does it run without RunComfy?
faq.answer: No. It requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and either login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Can it edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented endpoint accepts one to twenty input image URLs and can return one to four outputs per call.

faq.question: Are images processed locally?
faq.answer: No. The CLI submits prompts and image URLs to RunComfy, then downloads generated outputs from RunComfy domains.

faq.question: When should another model be used?
faq.answer: Use another model when the task is new image generation, multilingual in-image text editing, or a very precise single-reference local edit.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The workflow uses a third-party CLI and remote API, so users should protect tokens and avoid sending sensitive images without review.
